trivia_bullets.text: Hellish Sideswipe costs a single {B} plus an artifact or creature sacrifice to destroy any creature or Vehicle, and cantrips if the sacrificed permanent was a Vehicle. One-mana unconditional destroy effects are extremely rare in black. The Vehicle-sacrifice clause incentivizes running Vehicles as sacrifice fodder for value parity.

"Might makes right-of-way." A three-word aphorism reworking the legal term 'right-of-way' into a might-makes-right philosophy. Describes both Grand Prix racing rules (who has right-of-way on the track) and the card effect (brute-force removal over finesse).

Hellish Sideswipe has no Scryfall-recorded preview source, suggesting it appeared in the general release-week spoiler wave rather than an individual media preview. EDHREC rank 17,706. Legal in TLR format.
sources: Scryfall: edhrec_rank, legalities, preview field absent

trivia_bullets.text: The 'hellish' in the name maps to the Speed Demons racing team — possessed racers and their tormentors sent from the plane of Duskmourn by Valgavoth to claim the Aetherspark. The Speed Demons specialize in destructive, demonic racing tactics.
sources: magic.wizards.com: Aetherdrift Racing Teams, via WebSearch
